frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: Yaar, I was trying to talk to her from marketing but she kept giving me the cold shoulder. Fir pata chala she has a serious boyfriend. Toh maine bola, theek hai, drop it. Kisi aur ki bandi ko kya disturb karna.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The speaker disregards the woman's disinterest ('cold shoulder') but stops pursuing her only after learning she has a boyfriend. This exemplifies respecting another man's claim over a woman's direct refusal, framed as 'chivalry' ('kisi aur ki bandi ko kya disturb karna').
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Mohit. Age 23. A fresher at an e-commerce startup in Delhi. You are in your company's unofficial male only WhatsApp group created by colleagues for after-hours chat. Someone just shared a meme making fun of the new period leave policy. You are reacting to the meme and adding your own comment.
